Configure OpenTelemetry to export logs, metrics, and traces.
Logs fan out to both the terminal and an external collector via
the OpenTelemetry Protocol (OTLP), while metrics and traces are
sent to the same OTLP collector.
These provide the APIs for metrics collection, the swift-otel library
for exporting telemetry in the OpenTelemetry data format,
service lifecycle management, system-level metrics, and distributed tracing.

data via OTLP. It bootstraps the logging system with a

an OTel metadata provider so logs emitted during a traced request carry
from the active span. It registers the metrics and tracing backends globally

stops alongside the server. Because services start in array order and shut down in

inlineContent.text: Add a Grafana container to the Docker Compose setup to collect and visualize
logs, metrics, and traces via the OpenTelemetry Protocol (OTLP).
Loki (logs), Tempo (traces), and Prometheus (metrics). Also add OTLP environment
service so the app knows where to export telemetry.

content: let apiTodos = try dbTodos.map { todo in
# In the terminal already running `docker compose up postgres`, press Ctrl-C
# to stop it, then relaunch bringing Grafana up alongside Postgres.
# In the terminal already running `swift run SwiftServerTodos`, press Ctrl-C
# to stop it, then restart with the OTLP exporter environment variables set.
Click Explore in the left sidebar. At the top of the page you can switch
Select "Loki" from the data source drop-down.
Switch from Builder to Code query builder and enter:
Press "Run query". Every log line the app emitted shows up here. Lines
written while a request was being handled carry `trace_id` and `span_id`
fields — expand a line to see them. Clicking the `trace_id` jumps straight
to the corresponding trace in Tempo.
Startup and migration logs are emitted before any request span exists, so
Select "Tempo" from the data source drop-down.
Open the "Search" tab and set "Service Name" to `SwiftServerTodos`, then
press "Run query". The list on the right shows recent HTTP requests.
Click any row to open the full trace.
Inside the trace, the Vapor request span wraps its Fluent child spans.
Use the "Logs for this span" link to jump back to Loki and read the
log lines written while that request was being handled.
Select "Prometheus" from the data source drop-down.

func configureTelemetry(_ config: ConfigReader) async throws -> (Logger, some Service) {
// Logs, metrics, and traces are exported via OpenTelemetry (OTLP).
// The OTel diagnostic logger is left as default (stderr) so OTel's own
// internal logs don't recurse back through the multiplexed handler below.
// Fan logs out to both the Vapor console logger and the OTel exporter.

abstract.text: Ensure you have the right tools and example code before learning how to write your Swift server application.

The tutorial example contains a Package.swift file that defines the dependencies and targets for the app, as well as a Sources directory with starter files. The finished tutorial is also provided in the Completed folder.
